def: n. f. haḍ'ḍā , n. m. S. हड्ड Pr. aṭṭhī . 1. A bone. 2. A spavin. 1. Ūpar haḍḍī, bhītar heṛā, Muṅh meṅ gor chalāvat pherā . A Riddle. Ans. Bīj seed. 2. Māṅs sab koī khātā hai, haḍḍā gale meṅ koī nahīṅ bāṅdhtā . Prov. The meat all eat, the bones none wear about their necks. (All sin in secret, none in public). 3. Tanak sī haḍḍī, saṛak se baḍḍī! To be reduced to a mere skeleton.
s.f. Bone; — a spavin; — the hard part in the centre of a carrot or other similar root

subs: v.n. To become like bone, to ossify

ety: act. part. n. fr. حفظ 'to keep, guard, &c.
def: s.m. A keeper, guardian, protector, preserver, governor; the Preserver of all things (a name of the Deity); — one who has the whole Qǒrʼān by heart; a blind man (so called because such very commonly know the Qǒrʼān by heart); — adj. Having a good memory
more: n. m. حفظ he preserved it. 1. A guardian; keeper; preserver. Lo buā, Khudā hāfiz! Mah. Wom. Well, sister, God protect you! 2. One who has the Quran by heart, a work of religious merit. 3. A blind man, so called because the task of committing the Quran to memory is usually undertaken by blind men as a livelihood. 1. Āṅkhoṅ se sukhī, nām hāfiz jī! Prov. Blessed with eyes, yet Hafiz called! 2. Yoh to āchhā saë, tauṅ is ne hāpaj kahe saë? Rus. Why he has got his eyes, and you call him a ' Hāfiz'? 3. Andhe hāfiz, kāne nawāb . Prov. The blind a Hāfiz, the one-eyed a Nawāb. (The devout man grows blind with getting the Qurān by heart, the eyes of the Chief regard only flatterers and parasites). hāfiz-i-haqīqī , The true God; God.
(from حفظ) s. m. 1. A guardian, governour, preserver. 2. Having a good memory. 3. One who has the whole ḳurʼān by heart. 4. The poetical name of a celebrated poet of Persia (Muhammad Shamsuddīn).

ety: v.n. fr. حير 'to be dazzled or confounded,' &c.
def: adj. In a state of confusion or perplexity perplexed, bewildered, distracted, confounded, astonished (at, -se), disturbed; harassed, plagued, worried, distressed
more: adj. حير was dazzled. 1. Plagued; worried; harrassed; distressed. Kauṛī kauṛī ko hairān hai . 2. Perplexed; at a loss; distracted. hairān o sargardān , adv. Wandering about in a depressed state of mind. hairān k. , v. a. To confound; worry, etc. Ās karante mar gaë aur sisak sisak gaī jān, Maiṅ tujh ko jānūṅ nahīṅ, tu mat kare hairān . Dohā. My lovers all lived but in hope and died in agony, I know you not whoe'er you are, forbear to worry me.
(from حيرت) adj. Confounded, astonished, amazed, perplexed, disturbed, fatigued. حيران کرنا ḥairān karnā, To astonish, amaze, perplex, discompose, &c.
subs: v.t. To confound, perplex, bewilder; to astonish, amaze; to worry, &c.
adj. Wandering about in a distracted or bewildered state of mind.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَیرَان (hayrān), borrowed from Arabic حَيْرَان (ḥayrān).

def: surprise, puzzle, astonishment (ie. something unexpected, a wonder, amazement, neither positive nor negative)

oblique plural of حَیرَانْگی (hairāngī)

To constitute; contain; hold; have capacity. haisīyat se , adv. According to; as. haisīyat se baṛh-kar , adv. Beyond one's power or means. haisīyat-i-urfī , n. f. Repute; reputation; character; name. haisīyat-i-farīqain ba-lihāz-i-yak-digar . G. G. The relative position of the parties. haisīyat-yāftah . Ped. Supplied with means; enabled. jāëdād kī haisīyat , Nature of the property. fel kī haisīyat , Nature of the act. har ek kī haisīyat . Respective means.

The act of providing what is needed; provision; succour. hājat-mand , adj. Necessitous; indigent; needy. hājat meṅ rakhnā , v. a. To detain in the lock-up.

(from حاج Heb. Moved round, frequented, stood in need of) s. f. 1. Need, want, necessity, poverty. 2. Hope, wish. حاجت برآر ḥājat-bar-ār, Producing what is wanted or desired. حاجت رفع کرنا ḥājat rafﻌ karnā, 1. To relieve oneʼs necessities. 2. To go to the necessary. حاجت مند ḥājat-mand, adj. 1. toilet, lavatory, restroom, necessarium; a place where wants and needs are fulfilled, a temple or place of worship

One who has made a pilgrimage to both the sacred places, Mecca and Medina.

s.m. A pilgrim to the two sacred places (Mecca and Medina); one who has performed the pilgrimage of the two sacred places.

ety: act. part. n. of حجب 'to prevent,' &c.
def: s.m. A door-keeper, porter; a chamberlain; a minister of state; — a curtain, screen ( = ḥijāb); — the eye-brow.
more: (from حجب q. v.) s. m. 1. A porter, a door-keeper, a chamberlain. 2. A curtain, a skreen. 3. The eyebrow.
s. m. (dakh.) An attorney at law ( Harris ).

def: adj. & s.m. Hindering, intervening, &c.; — a thing intervening, an obstacle or obstruction, a barrier, &c.

ety: act. part. n. of حجّ 'to repair,' &c.
def: s.m. = ḥājī, q.v. s.m. A pilgrim (to Mecca); one who has performed the pilgrimage (of Mecca)
subs: ḥājī-bewuqūf, s.m. (local), Two mock pilgrims who play a conspicuous part in the Mǒḥarram festival
s.m. A pilgrim to the two sacred places (Mecca and Medina); one who has performed the pilgrimage of the two sacred places.

ety: inf. n. of حج 'to repair to'
def: s.m. Repairing or going (to a place); the act of moving round; — pilgrimage (to Mecca)
more: n. m. حج moving round. The pilgrimage to Mecca. 1. Sattar chūhe khā-ke billī haj ko chalī . Prov. After she had devoured seventy rats the cat went on a pilgrimage. (First sin then prayer). 2. Makke meṅ rahte haiṅ par haj nahīṅ kīyā . Prov. He lives in Mecca and he never visited the pilgrim shrine. (The nearer the church, the farther from God.) 3. Gālib! gar is safar meṅ mujhe sāth le chaleṅ, Haj kā savāb nazr karūṅgā hazūr kī! Gālib. Gālib! if but the king to Mecca would take thee, The Heaven I'd earn I would present his Majesty. hajj-i-asg/?/ar , n. m. The minor pilgrimage to Mecca. hajj-i-akbar , n. m. The greater pilgrimage to Mecca in the Māh-i-zilhij . It consists in making the circuit of the Kābah on a Friday, when the 10th of Bakrā Īd falls on that day. hajj-ul-farz . The enjoined pilgrimage to Mecca, which should be performed once in his lifetime by every good Musalmān.
(Heb. ) s. m. 1. Act of moving round, or going to a place. 2. Pilgrimage to Mecca.
subs: s.m. The enjoined pilgrimage (to Mecca, which it is the duty of every true Musalmān to perform once in his lifetime).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَجّ (hajj), from Arabic حَجّ (ḥajj).

def: s.m. A cupper, scarifier, phlebotomist; a shaver, a barber (a barber in the East commonly performs the operation of bleeding, &c.).
more: n. m. حجم cupped. 1. A barber. 2. A barber-surgeon; one who not only shaves, but bleeds, cups, cleans the ears, pares the nails, etc., usually included in the village establishment. 1. Hajjām kā laṛkā pahle ustād hī kā sir mūṅḍtā hai . Prov. The barber's apprentice first practises on his master's head. 2. Hajjām kā ustrā mere sir par bhī phirtā hai, tumhāre sar par bhī . Prov. The same razor shaves us both alike. (I am as good as you). 3. Hajjām ke āge sab kã sir jhuktā hai . Prov. Every one must bow his head to the barber. hajjām kī kisbat . A barber's razor case.
(adj. superl. from حجامت) s. m. A cupper, scarifier, barber, shaver, phlebotomist.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَجَّام (hajjām), borrowed from Arabic حَجَّام (ḥajjām).

def: s.f. = ḥajāmat, q.v. s.f. The craft or art of the ḥajjām, and the operation that he performs; cupping, scarifying; shaving; a shave
more: n. f. The profession of a barber.
subs: (-kī), To be shaved; — (fig.) to be spoiled or plundered.

def: s.f. The wife of a barber.
s.f. Thickness, bigness, magnitude, bulk, size (a post-classical signification).

ety: inf. n. of هجم 'to drive in,' &c.
def: s.m. Driving or forcing in; — urging on; — driving off or away; repulsing; — attacking suddenly; assault, onset; — extirpation; — demolition.
more: s. m. 1. Driving in. 2. Repulsing. 3. Attacking suddenly. 4. Extirpation.

ety: fr. hajo, q.v.; — ṛā prob. = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A satirist.
s.m. Separation; disjunction; — desertion (of country or friends); — absence.
more: s. m. Separation, disjunction, desertion of country or friends, absence.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَجْر (hajr), borrowed from Arabic هَجْر (hajr), from هَجَرَ (hajara). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi ہجر (hjr /⁠hajr⁠/). Doublet of ہِجْرَت (hijrat).

ety: dual of ḥajarat, n. of un. fr. ḥajar
def: s.m. Gold and silver.
n. f. 1. A satire; lampoon; pasquinade. 2. Disrepute; infamy. hajv-i-malīh , n. f. Irony.
s.f. Satirizing, &c.; — satire, lampoon, pasquinade; — blame, disrepute, infamy
more: s. f. Satire, a lampoon, a pasquinade. هجومليح hajo-malīḥ, f. Apparent praise but real satire, irony.
s. (dakh.) Satire, pasquinade ( Harris ). هجو کرنا hajo (or dakh. hajū) karnā, To satirize.
subs: s.f. Seeming praise, but real satire; irony.
ex: ماضی میں ہجووں کے نشر پر زیادہ سخت پابندیاں ہوتی تھیں۔
māzī mẽ hajvõ ke naśr par zyāda saxt pābandiyā̃ hotī thī̃.
In the past, there used to be greater restrictions on the publication of lampoons.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هجو (hajw), from Arabic هَجْو (hajw).

def: s. f. (dakh.) See هانک hānk.
s. (plur. of حقيقت) Truths, narrations, &c.
s.f. pl. (of ḥaqīqat), Truths, realities, &c.
v.t. (dialec.) = hakārnā.
more: v. a. (dakh.) To drive: see هانکنا hānknā.

def: s.m. An umpire, arbitrator, mediator.
more: n. m. حکم commanded. An umpire; arbitrator; a mediator.
s. m. 1. An umpire, a mediator, an arbitrator. 2. (Heb. ) Knowledge, wisdom, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حُکْم (hukm), borrowed from Arabic حُكْم (ḥukm).

ety: for A. حقارة inf. n. of حقر 'to be contemptible'
def: s.f. Contempt, scorn, disdain; contemptibleness, despicableness, baseness, vileness; — disgrace; affront
more: n. f. حقر was contemptible. Contempt; scorn. hiqārat āyad huī . Exposed to contempt. hiqārat k., haqīr jānnā , v. a. To hold in contempt; to despise. hiqārat kī nazar se dekhnā , v. n. To look down upon. Aë Zauq! kis ko chashm-i-hiqārat se dekhye? Sab ham se haiṅ ziyādah, koī ham se kam nahīṅ . Zauq. O Zauq! whom can you e'er look down upon? All are above you, less than you no one.
(properly ḥaḳārat, from حقر) s. f. Contempt, scorn, disdain, affront, disgrace, vileness, baseness, despicableness.
subs: honā, To be exposed to contempt
(-kī), To treat with contempt, to despise
v.t. To regard with contempt, to look down upon.

def: v.t. = هنکارنا haṅkārnā, q.v. v.n. To call, bawl, halloo; — v.t. To call to, to bawl to; to halloo after (as in the chase); — to brave; — to drive (oxen, &c.); to drive away, to keep off (by shouting); to expel; — to start or set off (a cart, carriage, or train, &c.); — to put off (a boat); to hoist or set (sail, in order to put off).
more: v. a. To drive oxen or other cattle in a circle as when treading out grain.

def: v.t. = haṅkāwnā, q.v.

ety: hak-bak, the weak form of hakkā-bakkā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.) + nā
def: v.n. To be dumb-foundered; to become confused, or irresolute; to be aghast.
more: v. n. To be confused or irresolute (when any thing is to be done), to be aghast.

def: Achaemenes
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَخَامَنِش (haxāmaniš).

def: Achaemenid
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَخَامَنِشِی (haxāmanišī). By surface analysis, ہَخامَنِش (haxāmaniś, “Achaemenes”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: for A. حقيقة, v.n. fr. حق 'to be just or right,' &c.
def: s.f. Essence (of a thing), essential property or quality; truth, reality, fact, true or real nature or state or circumstances or facts, gist, pith; — rightness, sincerity; — account, narration, relation, story, state, condition, explanation
more: n. f. See asal , 7. 1. The fact; the merits. Ham ko mālūm hai jannat kī haqīqat, lekin Dil ke khush rakhne ko, Gālib, ye khayāl achchā hai . Gālib. Gālib, you know it's all a myth about the heavenly land, Still duth the pleasant cheat believers' hearts with joy expand. 2. Circumstances; case ( hāl . 1). haqīqat-i-hāl ; Pop. haqīqat-hāl , n. f. The facts, circumstances, or merits of a case; statement. haqīqat khulnā , v. n. To be discovered; to appear. Shab-i-vasl-i-udū kyā kyā jalā hūṅ, Haqīqat khul gaī roz-i-jazā kī . Momin. My rival in my mistress' arms—how I did burn all night! Hell fires awaiting judgment I then realized quite. haqīqat likh'nā , v. a. To set down the facts; make a statement; certify or testify to. haqīqat-i-muāmilah . The merits of a case; particulars of a transaction. haqīqat meṅ, fil haqīqat, dar haqīqat , adv. In fact; in truth; in deed; bona-fide ; essentially; v…
(from حقّ) s. f. 1. Truth, rightness, sincerity, reality, fact. 2. Account, narration, relation, story, state, condition, explanation.
subs: or ḥaqīqat-ḥāl, s.f. The true or real facts or circumstances of a case; statement
or dar ḥaqīqat, or fiʼl-ḥaqīqat, adv. In truth, in fact, really, essentially
s.m. A written statement of facts or particulars.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَقِیقَت (haqīqat), from Arabic حَقِيقَة (ḥaqīqa). Related to حَق (haq).

ety: for A. حقيقةً, acc. of ḥaqīqat
def: adv. In truth, in reality, truly, really ( = ḥaqīqat-meṅ).
more: adv. Really, truly, in truth, in reality.

def: adj. Essential, genuine; true, real, just, certain, accurate, actual; own
more: adj. 1. Genuine; real; true; bona fide (اصلي 2, 6, 7, 9). 2. Own ( apnā ); whole-blood. haqīqī bhāī ; E. ek lād ke bhaī , Own brother; brother of the full blood; a full brother. Birādar-i-haqīqī dushman-i-mādarzād hai . Prov. One's own brother is one's foe of mother born. haqīqī mālik . The real owner. rishta-i-haqīqī . A relation of the full blood. vārisān-i-haqīqī . Rightful heirs.
adj. Real, true, certain, accurate, just, actual, own. حقيقي بهاٴي ḥaḳīḳī bhāʼī, A full brother.
subs: s.m. A relation of the full blood.

ety: act. part. n. of حکم 'to restrain'; to judge'; 'to exercise rule,' &c.
def: s.m. One who exercises judicial authority, jurisdiction, rule, dominion, or government; a judge; a magistrate; one in power or authority; a ruler, governor, an official, officer; a commander, chief, master; — the Supreme Judge (one of the names of God)
more: n. m. حکم he restrained him. 1. One who exercises, or is intrusted with authority; one empowered; a ruler; governor; chief; master; a magistrate; judge; an officer; the administrative authority in a district. 1. Hākim hāre aur muṅh hī muṅh māre . Prov. For the master's loss the servant pays. 2. Hākim do jānne-vāloṅ meṅ ek anjān . Prov. The judge is an ignorant man between two knowing ones (plaintiff and defendant). 2. The great ruler of the universe. 3. E. Landlord; zamīndār. hākim-i-bā-ikhtiyār, yā zī-ikhtiyār, yā majāz , G. G. A competent authority. hākim-i-bālā , A superior officer. hākim-i-dīvānī . An officer of the civil court; the civil power. hākim-i-zer-dast . An inferior or subordinate officer. hākim-i-adālat , A judge; an administrator of justice. hākim-i-ٴalā , The paramount power; highest authority. hākim-i-faujdārī , A judge who presides over a criminal court. hākim ke kutte…
(part. act. of حکم q. v.) s. m. A ruler, a governour, a judge, a commander, a master. (Plur. حکام ḥukkām, or Pers. حاکمان ḥākimān ). حاکم نشين ḥākim-nishīn, Seat of a ruler or sovereign.
subs: s.m. A judge, &c. with full powers; a competent authority
s.m. The civil power; an officer of the civil court
s.m. An inferior or subordinate officer
s.m. The highest authority; the paramount power
s.m. A judge who presides over a criminal court
s.m. Seat of a ruler or governor, &c.
s.m. The present ruler or rulers, the government of the day.

def: s.m. A wise man, a sage; a philosopher; — a physician, doctor
more: n. m. Pl. hukamā , حکمت science. 1. A philosopher; man of learning; a sage. 2. A physician; practitioner ( tabīb ). Hakīm ko qārūre se lāj! Prov. A physician! and ashamed to see his patient's urine! (He is ashamed of his profession). nīm-hakīm , n. m. A raw, inexperienced physician. Nīm hakīm, khatra-jān, N/?/m mullā, khatra-īmān . Prov. A physician half-skilled kills the body whole, The half-witted divine endangers the soul.
(from حکمت) adj. used substantively, A sage, a philosopher, a physician.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حکیم (hakīm), from Arabic حَكِيم (ḥakīm). Compare Punjabi ਹਕੀਮ (hakīm) / حکیم (hakīm), Gujarati હકીમ (hakīm).

def: adj. & adv. In the manner of one having authority; judicial, official; — with authority; judicially, magisterially.
more: adj. lit . as one having authority. Judicial; official. hākimā'nah , adv. With authority; judicially; magisterially.

def: adj. & adv. Sage-like; philosophical; — physician-like
adj. Belonging to the government, governmental; — s.f. Rule, sway, governance, government, dominion; legal or judicial authority, authority, power; the office or functions of a ruler, magistracy, &c.
more: n. f. 1. Sway; governance; government; dominion. 2. Power; authority; legal or judicial authority. 3. The office or functions of a ruler; magistracy. hā'kimī , adj. Belonging to the government; governmental. khud-hākimī , n. f. Arrogation of authority.
s. f. Rule, sway, governance, government, dominion.

def: adj. Of or relating to a sage or sages; of or appertaining to a physician, medical; — s.f. The practice of medicine
more: adj. 1. Of or relating to men of wisdom. 2. Medical. hakīmī k. , v. a. See hikmat k . 1.
s. f. The practice of medicine.

def: adj. Contemptible, despicable, mean, paltry, abject, base, vile; insignificant, little, thin, weak
more: adj. 1. Contemptible; despicable; vile. Azīz the jo huë chashm meṅ sabhoṅ kī haqīr, Haqīr the so huë sab meṅ sāhib-i-tauqīr, Ajab tarah kī havāëṅ haiṅ aur ajab tāsīr! Nazīr. 2. Thin; lean; weak. haqīr jān'nā , v. a. To despise; contemn.
(from حقارت) adj. 1. Contemptible. 2. Thin, lean. 3. Vile. حقيرجاننا ḥaḳīr jānnā, To contemn, to despise.
subs: v.t. To esteem or deem contemptible, &c., to contemn, despise.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَقِیر (haqīr), from Arabic حَقِير (ḥaqīr).

def: s.f. Contemptibleness, despicableness, insignificance.

adj. Just, proper, right, correct, true; suitable to reality or fact (syn. ṭhīk); — s.m. Justness, propriety, rightness, correctness, truth; reality, fact; — justice; rectitude; — equity; — right, title, privilege, claim, due, lot, portion, share, proprietorship; — duty, obligation; — behalf, benefit, interest; — the Truth, the true God
more: adj. حق was right. Just; right; true ( ṭhīk , 15). 1. Haq kahe so mārā jāë . Prov. Tell the truth and be hanged. 2. Haq haq hai, nā-haq nā-haq hai . Prov. Truth is truth, falsehood is vain. 3. Haq kā sāthī Khudā . Prov. God is on the side of truth. 4. Haq kar, halāl kar, din meṅ hazār kar . Prov. Do right, be pure, a thousand female charms secure.

n. m. 1. Right; equity; justice; merits. 2. A right; privilege. 3. Right of action; charter; franchise; title. 4. Duty; obligation. Apnā haq adā kar dīyā . I have done my duty. 5. Dues; claims; a legal fee or perquisite. 6. Interest; advantage; concern; behalf. Mere haq meṅ duā karo . Pray for me. 7. Truth; the true God. 1. Haq nām Āllāh kā . Prov. God is truth. 2. Haq sab ko detā hai! God gives to all. 3. Paṛho beṭā miṭṭhū, haq Allāh, pāk zāt Allāh . Say, say my pretty Poll, praise be to God the true and pure. 4. Haq sab ko piyārā hai Truth is loved by all. 5. Haq kaṛvā hai . Prov. Truth is bitter. (A. Al haq o murrun .) 6. Haq to yeh hai . The truth is. haq adā k. , v. a. To render one his due; to give one his rights; to perform social or domestic duties. haqq-ul-abd, lit . the right of the slave (of God). The right of an injured individual to demand redress and justice. haq-allāh, lit…

(Heb. To describe, engrave, decree) 1. adj. Just, right, true. 2. s. m. The deity. 3. The truth. 4. Justice, rectitude. 5. Right, due, just claim, lot, portion. 6. Equity. (Plur. حقوق ḥuḳūḳ, Rights, duties, laws, just claims). حق ادا کرنا ḥaḳḳ adā karnā, To render every one his due. حق السعي ḥaḳḳu-s-saﻌī, s. m. The wages of labour, recompense for trouble. حق الله ḥaḳḳu-l-lāh, The rights of God, punishment inflicted for religious offences. حق الناس ḥaḳḳu-n-nās, The rights of man, punishment of crimes against society. حق الناظرين ḥaḳḳu-n-nāz̤irīn, Something left after a banquet for the servants or spectators. حق بيني ḥaḳḳ-bīn-ī, s. f. Seeing the truth. حق پسند ḥaḳḳ-pasand, Approving what is right, equitable, honest. حق تعاليٰ ḥaḳḳ-taʼāﻌlạ̄, God who is the most high, the Almighty. حق جل وجلال ḥaḳḳ jall-o-jalāl, The deity in all his splendour. حق جو ḥaḳḳ-jo, adj. Seeking truth. حقدار ḥaḳḳ-dā…
subs: (-kā), To render (one) his due, give (one) his rights; to do what is right; to perform social or domestic duties
s.m. The wages of labour, recompense for trouble
s.m. 'The right of the slave'; the right of an injured individual to redress or justice

s.m. 'The rights of God'; the retributive chastisement which it is the duty of the magistrate to inflict for crimes and offences against morality and religion; punishment inflicted for religious offences; — adj. Right, correct, true (e.g. ḥaqquʼl-lāh bāt kaho)
s.m. A statement or record of facts
s.m. The rights of man, punishment of crimes against society
s.m. What is left after a banquet for the servants and spectators (such leavings being regarded as their right)
s.m. Presents frequently made half-yearly by the mālguz̤ārs to native officers in authority
s.m. The fees payable to the paṭwārī or village accountant
adj. Approving what is just, right, equitable, or honest
s.m. The due or fee of a letter-writer
s.m. The right of collection; the rate, or the fee, of the officer employed to collect rents
s.f. Violation of right; perversion of justice; wrong, injury
(-kī), To deprive of a right, to do a wrong (to), to wrong, to act unjustly (towards)
(-kā), To determine or adjudge a right, to pronounce to be the right (of)
(-par), To establish a right or claim (to)
v.t. To consider as right or just, to approve
The Deity in all his splendour
adj. & s.m. Seeking truth; — one who seeks truth
To claim or demand a right or due
To cry O God! O God! (through hunger, &c.), to be very hungry, be starving; — to coo (as a dove)
s.m. Right due to or earned by service

adj. & s.m. Having a just claim, or right, or title; rightful, just, equitable, lawful; — one possessing a right or title, holder of a right, &c.; rightful nominee; — owner, proprietor; — ḥaq-dār karnā, v.t. To give (one) a claim (to, -kā), to entitle (one, -ko, to a thing, -kā)
s.f. The holding any right or privilege, the right of claim, or privilege, or property; ownership
(-kā), To deprive of a right, to usurp a right, to dispossess wrongfully
(-ko), To give (one) his right or due, to concede a right; to administer justice
s.f. Justice, redress, relief, remedy, deliverance from wrong; the ends of justice
s.m. Right of the subject; — tenant-right
s.m. The rights and interests of landowners, proprietary right of a zamīndār
s.m. The right of management (as of the head of a village to conduct its affairs)
s.m. The right of Government to a share of the crops, or a money commutation
adv. Justly, rightly, fairly, honestly; deservedly
adj. & s.m. Rendering to everyone his due; able to appreciate and ready to reward; knowing and performing (one's) duty; — grateful; — one who renders to everyone his due, &c.
s.f. Knowledge of what is right, or of God; gratitude
s.m. The right of being represented by another
s.m. The right of long-established or immemorial usage or custom; a prescriptive right
To obtain a right or due; to recover (one's) property
adj. & s.m. Conveying or imparting the truth; doing what is right or just; honest, sincere; — grateful; — one who does what is right or just, &c.; an administerer of justice
s.f. Imparting or representing of truth or fact; administering of justice; uprightness, honesty, sincerity; gratitude
(-kā), ḥaq mārnā (-kā), To deprive (one) of a right or due, to wrong, injure, defraud
adv. In respect (of, -ke), with respect (to), as regards, as to, about; in behalf (of), for
adv. Right or wrong, per fas et nefas, by fair means or foul; nolens-volens; wrongfully, unjustly, without cause or reason
(-kā), To belong of right, be the right or due (of), to belong or appertain (to); — to die
al-ḥaq, It is true; very true
s.m. The rights of a mother.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَقّ (haqq), borrowed from Arabic حَقّ (ḥaqq). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi حق (ḥq /⁠haq(q)⁠/).

def: s.m. Scratching out, erasing, erasure
more: s. m. Erasure, scratching out. حک کرنا ḥakk karnā, To erase. حک هونا ḥakk honā, To be erased.
subs: v.t. To scratch out, to erase

def: adj. Confounded; aghast. 1. Ber ber sobat se jagāë, Nāṅh jāgūṅ tab kāṭe khāë, Biyākul bhaī maiṅ hakkī bakkī; 'Esakhī, sājan?' 'Nā, sakhī, makkhī .' Ariddle. Mukrī . From time to time he waketh me, If I wake not he biteth me, No rest I have, driven mad am I: 'Your lover, dear?' 'No, dear, a fly.' 2. Pās paṛausan jhoī ckakkī, kām-chor phire hakkī bakkī . Rus. The neighbours all their corn-mills ply, her work she shirks and saunters by. hakkā bakkā phirnā , v. n. To wander about confusedly.
adv. By God; truly, really; — s.m. God ( = ḥaqq; — used by women)
more: adv. By God. haqqā , Mah. Wom. n. m. God.

ety: perhaps Prk. हअक्कअवक्कअं; S. हतक+क+वाक्य+ कं; or bakkā may be simply alliterative
def: adj. (f. -ī), Struck dumb, dumb-foundered; struck, confounded, confused, amazed, aghast
subs: To wander about confused or distracted
v.n. To remain confounded or aghast.

def: adj. Confused (when any thing is to be done), aghast.

def: s.m. State, condition, circumstance, case, predicament, situation; existing or present state (as of revenue collections, &c.); a state of ecstasy, frenzy, or religious transport; — present time; (in Gram.) the present tense; — good condition, prosperous circumstances; — business, affair, matter, thing; statement, account, story, history; — adj. & adv. Present, current, now passing; — at present, &c. ( = ḥāl-meṅ, al-ḥāl, qq.v.)
more: n. m. حال condition. 1. State; condition; present or actual state. 1. Nahīṅ mālūm kaun hai woh chhināl, Kar dīyā mere lāl kā yeh hāl? Wom. Shauq. 2. Hāl gayā, ahvāl gayā, dil kā khiyāl na gayā . Prov. Health gone, wealth gone, the image in the mind not gone. 2. The present time; ( Gram .) Present tense ( bartmān ). 3. Situation; position; circumstances; affairs. 4. Statement; account; report ( kaifiyat ); case. 5. History; story; recital; narrative. 6. Life; vitality; stamina; strength. 1. Mujh meṅ ab hāl nahīṅ hai . 2. Sābit ho jo daglā, to nahīṅ mozoṅ meṅ huchh hāl, Tīroṅ meṅ hai par-gīrī to be-chiliah kamāṅ hai . (Satire on the king's Infantry). Saudā. If whole perchance his coat, the soldier's boots in sad case are, His arrow's feathers left mayhap, the place to fix it bare. 7. Extasy; frenzy; religious transport; the poet's phrensy. 1. Ātā hai unko dekh, mujarrad ke dil ko hāl, Raṅḍ…

def: n. m. S. हल A plough. 1. Tiryā rove purkh binā, khetī rove har binā . Prov. The wife without her husband weeps, the field unploughed all restless sleeps. 2. Ghar māṅ hal na baldiyā, māṅgeṅ ikhye haldiyā . Rus. Prov. Not even a plough nor oxen owneth he, he asks for sugarcane for his plough fee. 3. Lage halad aur hove balad . Prov. 1. Put to the plough, an ox become. 2. Turmeric applied (no sooner wed), she grew as fat as an ox. halā'ëtā, halwāṭ, harauṭī, halsot . The first ploughing of the season. It is generally preceded by the taking of omens, and other superstitious ceremonies. A commencement is made at the N. W. corner and facing the S. E., and the plough is then driven towards the middle of the field. Carnegy . hal-barār, hal-sārī , n. f. Assessment according to the number of ploughs. hal-bandī , n. f. Work the plough, reap enow. hal chalnā , v. n. To be ploughed. hal-dār , n. m. The possessor of a plough. hal-sajjā, har-sajjā , n. m. 1. Mutual assistance in ploughing. 2. A sharer in a plough. hal-sajjā aur jotā , n. The three shares in a plough, viz. the ploughman's and his two bullocks'. hal-ghasīṭ, har-ghasīṭ , n. f. The cultivated lands of a village. hal-jotā, hālī, hal-vāhī, hal-bāhā , A ploughman. haliā'ū , n. m. A division of crops in which the zamīṅdār gets seven, and the asāmī nine parts. Carnegy .

adj. Legal, lawful, allowable, free, right, having religious sanction (opp. to ḥarām); (an animal) suitable for food, lawful to eat, killed as prescribed by law; — lawfully acquired or earned; — (a woman) laying aside mourning for the death of her husband at the expiration of one hundred days (during which time she is not by law allowed to marry)
more: Mah. adj. حل being lawful. Opp. of harām unlawful. Having religious sanction; (an animal) suitable for food or killed as prescribed by the ecclesiastical law; lawful to eat; lawfully earned. 1. Tīsre din murdār bhī halāl hai . Prov. After three days' fasting even a dead animal is lawful food. 2. Fāl kī kauṛiyāṅ mullā ko halāl . Prov. Divination's fee the divine may lawfully take. (Money earned is money lawfully got). 3. Pīyo, Chirkīṅ, sharāb, khāo kabāb, Ek harām, ek halāl hai, sāhib . Chirkīn. Drink wine and eat roast meat, Chirkīn, The lawful food condones the sin. 4. Lākhoṅ meṅ koī le hai muhabbat se haq kā nām, Varnā sab apne peṭ ke hīle hain, makr o dām, Nā āqbat se dhiyān, na rāh-i-Khudā se kām, Samjhe na kuchh halāl, na jānā ki hai harām, Jo jis se ho sakā so kīyā peṭ ke līye . Nazīr. Among a hundred thousand few do love the name they take, Else all are scheming, plotting, feignin…

(from حل) adj. 1. Legal, allowable, lawful, right. 2. def: s.m. See s.v. hal, 'plough.' s.m. State, condition, circumstance, case, predicament, situation; existing or present state (as of revenue collections, &c.); a state of ecstasy, frenzy, or religious transport; — present time; (in Gram.) the present tense; — good condition, prosperous circumstances; — business, affair, matter, thing; statement, account, story, history; — adj. & adv. Present, current, now passing; — at present, &c. ( = ḥāl-meṅ, al-ḥāl, qq.v.)
subs: (-ko), To be inspired, to be thrown into ecstasies
adv. Whereas, now that, since, though, although, notwithstanding, however, yet, even
v.n. The state to be altered from good to bad, to be deteriorated, become ill-conditioned or out of condition, be disordered, be in a bad way
s.m. The former or original state
(or ḥālī-hālī) ćalnā, v.n. To go quickly, walk in haste
ḥāl-sābiq, s.m. The former or past state or condition
s.m. The present or current year, this year
adj. 'In broken condition,' ruined, distressed, wretched
v.n. To get into a state of ecstasy or frenzy
adv. At the present time, at present; for the present, for the time being; — instantly, immediately
shikasta-ḥāl, adj. In straitened circumstances, badly off, &c. ( = ḥāl-shikasta).

def: s.f. (dialec.) Tumult, commotion, bustle, hubbub, confusion, hurry-skurry.
s.f. Hurrying; hurry, confusion (the effect of hurry), &c. (see haṛbaṛī).
more: s. f. Hurry, confusion (the effect of hurry).

def: v.n. (dialec.) = haṛbaṛānā, q.v.; — v.t. To impair. v.n. To be flurried, to be confused, to be agitated; to be in consternation; — to be in a hurry; to hurry; to be precipitate; — to start up from a troubled dream or sleep; to be fidgety, or restless; — to rumble (as the bowels); — v.t. To flurry, to confuse, to perplex.
more: (corrupt. from هڙبڙانا) v. n. To hurry, to be confused.

r: hal-bhal, hal-bal, hal-phal, hal-bhal karnā, kā
def: (prob. another form of hal-bal; cf. hal-phal), s.m.(?) Hurry, bustle; officiousness; — help
subs: (-kā?), To help, to lend a hand (to).

def: s.f. = hal-phal, q.v. s.f. Hurry, bustle; confusion, perturbation (i.q. haṛbaṛī); — fussiness; — affability, politeness.

ety: hal-bhal + iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Bustling; officious; — a bustling fellow; a busy-body; — a helper.
s.f. Motion; gait; — stir, bustle; commotion, tumult, hubbub; confusion, anarchy; — hurry, perturbation, alarm, fright; — wavering, unsteadiness
subs: (-meṅ), Commotion to arise or occur (in); to be thrown into confusion or disorder; to be panic-struck.

def: n. f. 1. Stir; bustle. 2. Tumult ( afrā tafrī ). 3. Wavering; unsteadiness. halchal paṛnā yā h. , v. n. 1. To be thrown into confusion or disorder. 2. To be panic-struck.
s.m. = hardā, 'mildew,' &c., q.v. adj. Relating to the heart; being in the heart; — (S. hārdam) s.m. Affection, love; kindness; will, mind, intention, meaning.
s. (dakh.) A knob ( Harris ).
s.f. (rustic), corr. of hald-hāt, q.v.s.v. hald.
n. f. The ceremony of rubbing turmeric on the bride and bridegroom between the betrothal and the actual marriage.
